schemas.name: How to Get a Free Healthcare Facility Cleaning Assessment in Massachusetts
schemas.description: Simple 4-step process to schedule a free healthcare facility cleaning walkthrough with Dory's Cleaning Services in Massachusetts.

step.name: Request Your Free Facility Assessment
step.text: Call (978) 307-8107 or fill out the online form. We respond within 24 hours.

step.name: Schedule a 20-Minute Walkthrough
step.text: We schedule a facility tour at a time that does not disrupt your patient flow.

step.name: Receive Hospital-Grade ATP Surface Testing
step.text: We test 4 high-touch surfaces using ATP bioluminescence verification - the same technology used in major hospitals.

step.name: Get Your Written Compliance Report
step.text: Receive a detailed pass/fail report you can keep, share with your team, and use for accreditation surveys.

mainEntity.name: What healthcare facilities does Dory's Cleaning Services in Massachusetts serve?

acceptedAnswer.text: We serve medical offices, specialty clinics (cardiology, dermatology, dental, urgent care), ambulatory and outpatient facilities, rehabilitation and nursing facilities, and healthcare administrative offices across 296 cities in Massachusetts including Boston, Cambridge, Worcester, Springfield, Lowell, Salem, and more.

mainEntity.name: How much does healthcare facility cleaning cost in Massachusetts?

acceptedAnswer.text: Healthcare cleaning pricing varies based on facility size, patient volume, frequency (daily, weekly, on-call), and required service scope. Typical investments range from $75 to $300+ per cleaning visit. Call (978) 307-8107 for a free customized quote based on your specific Massachusetts facility.

mainEntity.name: Are you licensed and insured for healthcare facility cleaning in Massachusetts?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Dory's Cleaning Services holds Massachusetts Home Improvement Contractor License #213341 and carries $2,000,000 in liability insurance. Our staff is certified in OSHA Bloodborne Pathogens (29 CFR 1910.1030), Infection Control, and ATP Bioluminescence Verification Testing.

mainEntity.name: What is ATP bioluminescence testing and why does it matter?

acceptedAnswer.text: ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate) bioluminescence testing measures organic residue on surfaces in 10 seconds. It is the same technology used by infection prevention programs in major U.S. hospitals. The test provides objective, numerical proof that surfaces are truly disinfected — not just visually clean. We offer free ATP verification assessments to qualifying Massachusetts healthcare facilities.

mainEntity.name: Do you provide compliance documentation for Joint Commission and DPH inspections?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Every cleaning visit is logged with date, time, areas serviced, EPA-registered disinfectants used, contact times, and quality control verification. Documentation supports Massachusetts Department of Public Health (DPH) inspections, Joint Commission surveys, AAAHC accreditation, OSHA audits, and internal quality reviews.

mainEntity.name: How does healthcare cleaning differ from regular janitorial services?

acceptedAnswer.text: Standard janitorial cleans for visual appearance. Healthcare facility cleaning requires evidence-based protocols designed to break the chain of infection transmission: EPA-registered hospital-grade disinfectants applied with proper contact times, clean-to-dirty sequencing, color-coded microfiber to prevent cross-contamination, OSHA-compliant biohazard handling, and documented verification. The cost of inadequate cleaning is measured in healthcare-associated infections (HAIs), regulatory fines, and patient safety incidents.

mainEntity.name: How quickly can you start cleaning my Massachusetts healthcare facility?

acceptedAnswer.text: After your free facility walkthrough, we typically can begin scheduled cleaning within 1-2 weeks. For urgent biohazard incidents or emergency disinfection situations, we offer 24/7 on-call response across Massachusetts. Call (978) 307-8107 or email contact@doryscleaningservices.com.

Why Your Facility Needs Clinical-Grade Environmental Cleaning

Dory's Cleaning Services is a Massachusetts-based commercial healthcare cleaning company founded by a clinical cleaning professional with more than 22 years of experience working inside clinical environments including emergency trauma, cardiology, ambulatory care, procedural departments, nursing facilities, and MedSurg units.

We provide structured, clinical-grade sanitation services designed exclusively for licensed commercial healthcare facilities. We do not serve residential properties. Our focus is infection prevention, environmental safety, and operational reliability for medical and care facilities across Massachusetts.

After years of witnessing inconsistent sanitation standards and the compliance risks they create for facility administrators, staff, and patients, Dory's was built to bring infection prevention accountability and proper documentation into commercial healthcare cleaning.

This is not basic environmental cleaning service.
This is clinical-grade commercial healthcare cleaning.

Clinical Cleaning Expertise

Why Clinical Experience Matters in Healthcare Facility Environmental Cleaning

Dory's Cleaning Services was founded with one goal: to bring infection prevention awareness and accountability to commercial healthcare cleaning. Founded by a professional with 22+ years of clinical healthcare experience, giving us insight into the operational realities and compliance risks inside healthcare facilities.

How It Works

Simple 4-Step Process

Getting your healthcare facility professionally sanitized is straightforward. Here is how we deliver compliance-driven results.

1

Request a Facility Assessment

Call us or fill out our form. We will schedule an on-site walkthrough to understand your facility's sanitation and compliance needs.

2

Compliance Review & Proposal

We assess your facility's compliance requirements and create a customized sanitation protocol with documented procedures.

3

We Sanitize Your Facility

Our trained environmental cleaning professionals execute your sanitation protocol using EPA-registered disinfectants and CDC/OSHA-compliant procedures.

4

Compliance Documentation Delivered

Receive detailed cleaning verification logs and quality control reports supporting your facility's regulatory and accreditation requirements.
